one_line_ask: Take my mixed-lighting packaging photos, color-grade them into one cohesive polished hero set, then assemble a fast-paced 16:9 website sizzle reel plus a square social cut and a moodboard.
brief:
I run a small packaging design studio and I'm launching a new website. I have a folder of finished work — six photographs/mockups of packaging projects I've shipped (a tea carton, a cosmetics jar, a coffee bag, a craft-beer can, a perfume box, and a chocolate bar sleeve), all shot in my studio over the past year so they don't match: some are warm, some are cool, a couple are slightly crooked, exposure drifts shot to shot. I also have one clean photo of a plain kraft box I want to use as the title card, but it's framed too tight — I need the canvas extended so I can overlay my logo on negative space.

I want all of this pulled together into an eye-catching HERO sizzle reel for the top of my homepage: fast-paced, energetic, and making my design work look its absolute best. Please do the following, in order, so the look is cohesive across every shot:

1) Bring all my supplied photos in and get them straight and uniformly exposed (auto-straighten the crooked ones, auto-tone, lift the dark portions a touch).
2) Give the whole set one consistent grade — neutralize the mixed white balance toward a clean studio look, push vibrance so the print colors pop, apply a single cinematic Lightroom
